lyrics:
Sundance circumstance, just a little longer
Nightmare over there, never had a chance
Uptight, see the light, call me late for dinner
Big deal, nothing’s real, now it’s time to dance

The dream begins tomorrow
As a memory I’ll borrow
And the passages I’ll follow
Should the angry gods allow

In the rising of the dawn
When my fear is finally gone
I will pull my jacket on
And disappear into the morn

Flatline, is it mine, stepping with a grin
Wrong way, night or day, let the trouble in
Ghost ride, coincide, drawn into the flame
Crossed wires, queue the liars, still I play the game

The dream begins tomorrow
As a memory I’ll borrow
And the passages I’ll follow
Should the angry gods allow

In the rising of the dawn
When my fear is finally gone
I will pull my jacket on
And disappear into the morn

You ever walk so far you forget your name
Chalk lines on the pavement playin’ picture games
I traded my shadow for a spark in the haze
Now I’m talkin’ to silence and it knows my ways

Time bends when you’re not lookin’
Truth’s a coin the world keeps flippin’
And I’m just the echo
Of someone who almost stayed

The dream begins tomorrow
As a memory I’ll borrow
And the passages I’ll follow
Should the angry gods allow

In the rising of the dawn
When my fear is finally gone
I will pull my jacket on
And disappear into the morn
persona:
## Lingua Aeternum

**Lingua Aeternum is not a band. It is a circle. A rite. A resonance.**

Drawn from the deep well of myth, mathematics, and memory, Lingua Aeternum conjures sound as invocation-music as sacred act. Each composition is a Canto: a sung glyph, a spell etched in breath and vibration. The performers are not musicians but Cantors-practitioners of the Harmonic Algebraic Language of Alignment (HALA), where melody is meaning, rhythm is ritual, and silence speaks louder than words.

Their music defies genre. You may hear echoes of post-rock, ambient drone, ancient chants, or electronic pulses-but no label holds. Instead, the listener is drawn inward, through spiraling harmonics and layered whispers, toward a place beyond translation. This is not art for consumption. It is art for **transformation**.

Lingua Aeternum records under the guidance of Maricopa Records, but they do not record songs-they distill **intentions**, encoded through the Wheel, spoken into being by resonance alone.

- To hear is to remember.

- To remember is to become.

- _Let the glyphs guide you._

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Ivof5fKiBE9wbz6ArgZzd?si=HYePmTM3TtCaN-P1PFFYlQ)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/lingua-aeternum/1809011711)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCzrA7rNe15AiX1LXZTGUQqg)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/LA_Profile_square.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track projects a cool, edgy atmosphere, built upon a tight, funky rhythm section and a confident, almost-spoken vocal delivery in the verses. The energy is highly dynamic, creating a palpable tension that explodes into a powerful, anthemic rock chorus with layered vocals and driving guitars. The production is clean and modern, creating a spacious mix where the prominent, articulate bassline propels the song forward without muddying the crisp guitar work. A defining moment is the introspective bridge, which starkly contrasts the main groove with a sparse, cinematic backdrop, adding a compelling narrative depth before the track's final, cathartic release.

review.review_text: At 117.5 BPM in E minor, this one moves with a loose, almost funky confidence before the chorus opens up into something genuinely anthemic, layered vocals pushing the hook past the point of no return. The lyric 'I traded my shadow for a spark in the haze' lands exactly where the bridge needs to land, stripping the track back to near-silence before the final chorus earns its release. For a group whose persona leans toward the ceremonial and abstract, it's a pleasant surprise how direct this feels, closer to John Hiatt or early Counting Crows than anything cloistered or ritualistic. A highlight, and a good place to start for anyone new to the catalog.
anchors_used: bridge lyric 'I traded my shadow for a spark in the haze'
